title: Spain
content:
How to DistinguishThe country-code domain is .esPedestrian-warning signs show crosswalks with 8 stripes — only Spain and Andorra use this designRed warning triangles (aside from STOP) have no outer borderBollards often use yellow reflectors“Calle” means street/road in Spanish; in Catalonia you may see “Carrer” insteadChevron signs exist with black or blue backgroundsPostal boxes are yellow cylinders branded with the Correos logoRoad markings are mainly white, though yellow paint appears occasionallySigns you can findRepresentative Companies説明はページ下部。必ずしも道端で見つかるとは限りません。
Correos mailboxes are tall yellow cylinders marked with the Correos logo (参考文献 Sociedad Estatal Correos y Telégrafos, S.A.).
“CALLE” means “street” in Spanish. In Catalonia, the Catalan word “CARRER” appears instead. The sign on the right comes from Catalonia’s Free Software Street (参考文献 Free Software Street).
Pedestrian-warning signs typically show 8 stripes on the crosswalk . Red warning signs (except STOP) lack an outer border and are mounted on slim rectangular pipes . Here is the rear view link .
Many bollards share the same yellow scheme as the mailboxes . Some designs vary slightly . Similar bollards also appear in Chile and Argentina (Jujuy) , so avoid guessing exclusively from bollards.
Small orange or yellow kilometer plates often sit along the roadside, showing the route number .
Chevron signs exist with black or blue backgrounds .
Highways frequently include short guardrail segments painted white with black stripes .
Narrow down state/regionLarge pine forests appear across Castilla y León and AragónOlive groves dominate AndalusiaMediterranean provinces show Aleppo pines, stone pines, and scrubby coastsCastilla y León and Aragón feature vast pine forests .
Aleppo pine woods with sandy soil are also common near the Mediterranean coast .
Andalusia is blanketed by olive groves laid out in a grid . Expect rolling hills with repeated tree rows.
Coastal Catalonia and Valencia mix Aleppo pines, stone pines, and scrubby cliffs .
CitiesMadrid and Barcelona have multi-lane boulevards with palm trees or plane treesValencia, Málaga, and Sevilla share Mediterranean coastal architecture<ul class="rule-list"><li>Examples with Street View references:<ul><li>MadridBarcelona Sevilla Almería province is famous for greenhouse farming; the southeastern shoreline looks white from thousands of plastic houses . The region is nicknamed the “Sea of Plastic” (参考文献 Almería province).
Outside of the three areas shown in the reference map, rice paddies are scarce (参考文献 European Rice Cropland Mapping with Sentinel-1 Data: The Mediterranean Region Case Study).
IndustryIn addition to conventional photovoltaic farms , Spain hosts tower-type solar-thermal plants and parabolic-trough facilities . Some plants lack nearby roads, so they are easier to spot on satellite imagery than in rounds.
Narrow down city/townGreenhouse farming blankets southeastern AlmeríaVillages tucked between limestone massifs appear near Picos de Europa National Park(参考文献 Picos de Europa National Park)Spain also maintains the North African enclaves of Melilla and CeutaBy El Jim - https://flic.kr/p/4Kwtmh, CC BY 2.0, Link

title: Sri Lanka
content:
How to DistinguishCountry-code top-level domain is .lkSignage features Sinhala (සිංහල) and Tamil (தமிழ்)Rear license plates are yellowMany signposts use poles painted with black-and-white stripesWhite-and-black curb blocks line waterways and intersections (by plonk it )Some coverage shows a blue-white-red Google car (by Geotips )Utility poles often have rectangular cut-outs and oversized coilsSinhala (සිංහල) and Tamil (தமிழ்) appear together as official languages. Poles with black-and-white striping are also common.

Sinhala and Tamil are both widely used.

title: Sudan
content:
How to DistinguishThe country domain is .sd.Most of the country consists of plains lying below roughly 600 m in elevation (参考文献 Embassy of Japan in Sudan – Climate, Topography, Languages).
Fertile zones along the Nile support crops such as wheat , while the northern reaches are arid and see very little agriculture (参考文献 USDA Sudan Production).

title: Svalbard
content:
How to DistinguishThe country domain is .sj.License plates appear on a black background with faint orange characters.Buildings use cold-climate architecture and color palettes.Boats are a common mode of transport.Because of the severe tundra climate—similar to Greenland—homes are rarely built directly on the ground. Gaps often separate the house from the permafrost surface . Only short, needle-like grasses grow; trees struggle to survive.
Snowmobiles are sometimes parked right in front of homes .
License plates feature orange characters on a black background.
Narrow down state/regionBarentsburg is almost entirely Russian, so Russian signage is common.Pyramiden has virtually no permanent residents but is being redeveloped for tourism.In 1932 the Dutch sold the coal rights to the Soviet Union, and a Russian state firm has managed the town ever since. After Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, reports say Ukrainians left (参考文献 Баренцбург). The town feels dark overall with aging buildings .
Sweden sold the coal rights to the Soviet Union in 1927. Mining ceased in the 1990s and the town was abandoned, but tourist lodges and hotels are being restored, giving it a slightly tidier appearance .

title: Sweden
content:
How to DistinguishThe domain is .seLane-edge dashed lines on the shoulder are very shortNordic edge linesThe pedestrian-warning sign shows a crossing with four stripesDeep red (Falu red) timber houses are iconicArrow-shaped direction signs are distinctive—less like a perfect ▷(from 『【GeoGuessr】公認プレイヤーによる国当て完全解説！ - Daig_O』)Street names often end with gatan (“street”) or vägen (“road”)Signs you can findSweden and Norway are full of log houses painted in deep red (Falu red) .
The pedestrian crossing sign has four stripes. From left to right: Finland, Norway, Sweden.
Swedish direction arrows have a unique silhouette—noticeably different from the neat ▷ used in neighbouring countries(from 『【GeoGuessr】公認プレイヤーによる国当て完全解説！』).
SwedenFinlandNorwayChevron boards use the national colours (yellow/blue). Norway and Finland use yellow on black, so they are easy to distinguish (参考文献 Map of European Road Curve Chevron Signs).
Route numbers appear on yellow background signs with an inset version of the national flag colours .
Beware of confusing Sweden with the Åland Islands (Finland), which use Swedish language and similar signage.
Street names ending with gatan (“street”) or vägen (“road”) point to Swedish. Street blades — even “gatu” forms — are rare elsewhere.
One-way zones are labelled “Enkelriktad zon”. Compare the wording across the Nordic languages:

title: Taiwan
content:
How to DistinguishThe domain is .twRoadside bollards with red dots(by Geotips )Major convenience stores like Hi-Life and OK MartRed and yellow lines are used on roadsDiagonal striped utility polesConcrete block walls and retaining walls are commonSigns you can findRepresentative Companies説明はページ下部。必ずしも道端で見つかるとは限りません。

Roads often use red or yellow lines . Like Korea, utility poles use diagonal stripes .
Concrete block walls and retaining walls are common .

If the bollards and utility poles are angular, it may be Thailand. However, please note that similar angular bollards can be found in Laos, and you can find angular utility poles in Sri Lanka and the Philippines. If the license plate is yellow, the coils on the utility poles are large, or there are holey poles, you may be thinking of Sri Lanka, and if your car has a greenish license plate number or a unique-shaped vehicle, you may be thinking of the Philippines.

In Thailand, driving is on the left side of the road. Most ordinary passenger cars have white license plates, and cabs have yellow license plates. In Laos, standard cars are yellow and drive on the right side of the road.

title: U.S. Virgin Islands
content:
How to DistinguishThe country domain ends with .vi.The Google car is distinctive.Mostly flat terrain with many straight roads points to the low-lying southern island, St. Croix.Hilly terrain with sparse population and dense greenery suggests the northeastern island, St. John.Hilly terrain with a more built-up feel indicates the northwestern island, St. Thomas.On the northern islands the vehicles are often white and the landscape is hilly. If it looks lively with plenty of residents or tourists, you are likely on St. Thomas in the northwest .
If the northern island feels remote, with white cars, few houses, and lots of greenery, consider the northeastern island of St. John. During the era of the transatlantic slave trade it produced sugarcane, and the Annaberg Sugar Plantation ruins remain, but the population today is very small .
Until 2016 light-blue license plates were used . Dark blue plates can also appear.
Lower image by User:Mattes – Self-photographed, CC BY 2.0 de, Link
The southern island, St. Croix, is the flattest and most populated (参考文献 St. Croix). Straight highways usually mean you are on the southern island.

title: Uganda
content:
How to DistinguishLeft-side drivingDomain is .ugHouses are basically made of wood and brick, often with curtains at the entrance.Houses are made of wood and brick
The larger yellow license plate on the back of the car. Also, the van with light blue stripes on the left side is a cab (or a shared-ride bus), which is also often seen on the road(参考文献 Economic and Social Position of Boda Boda (Motorcycle Taxis) in Urban Uganda).
A white car or a car with metal bars. If you see a bar, you are in a national park in the suburbs, not in the capital.
There are 笘 symbols on the poles.
Narrow down city/townNational parks have things like Euphorbia ingens and termite mounds on the roadside(参考文献 Euphorbia ingens).Euphorbia ingens can be found in Queen Elizabeth National Park and elsewhere.

title: Ukraine
content:
How to DistinguishCountry-code top-level domain is .uaPedestrian warning signs show three zebra barsThe Google car sometimes reveals a faint red hood (by Geotips )Letters such as і, ї, є, ґ exist in Ukrainian but not in Russian; conversely, Russian uses ы, э, ё insteadLicense plates feature blue and yellow bands on the leftSigns you can findSpotting characters like і, ї, є, ґ confirms Ukrainian, whereas letters such as ы, э, ё appear in Russian but not Ukrainian.
License plates often show blue and yellow bands on the left, though heavy blur can make them difficult to read.
By Andrii crimea ua at Ukrainian WikipediaW - Own work by the original uploader, CC BY-SA 3.0, Wikimedia Commons
You may glimpse a muted red hue from the Google car. (by Geotips ) Russia and Belgium also have red-tinted cars, and some European imagery features red fringing, so do not rely on this cue alone.
Left: Ukraine. Right: Russia. (Displayed via Chrome DevTools “Enumerate vision deficiencies Emode.) If distinguishing red shades is difficult, lean on other clues.Narrow down state/regionThe north and west have a humid continental climate (Dfb) with ample rainfall, while the southeast is dry steppe (BSk). Expect denser, taller forests toward the northwest and sparser, lower vegetation and farmland in the southeast. (参考文献 Embassy of Japan in Ukraine — Country overview (Japanese))Compare roads in the northwest versus the southeast. Even in the northwest you can still find open plains, so regional identification remains tricky.
Narrow down city/townStreet View covers areas around the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant (参考文献 Chornobyl Nuclear Power Plant (Japanese))Expect many abandoned or decaying buildings throughout the exclusion-zone imagery.

title: United Arab Emirates
content:
How to DistinguishCountry-code top-level domain is .aeLicense plates are mainly white but may have a red strip on the leftDubai’s skyline features prominent high-risesThe Google Car roof is sometimes visible Signs you can findRoad signs and route shields have a distinctive look. In some areas you can spot the Google Car’s white roof.
Expect high-rise silhouettes in major cities such as Dubai. Construction sites are common.
Unlike Qatar, terrain in the Emirates is rarely perfectly flat. Eastern suburbs reveal rocky hills —beware confusion with the Omani exclave of Musandam.
License plates are mainly white, but a red strip may appear on the left. Qatar uses similar colours, so confirm with other clues.
Signposts often have striped end poles . Simple silver poles exist too, so use context.
The Google Car is distinctive—often a white vehicle with the roof visible. Even when the roof isn’t visible, expect a white body.
Narrow down state/regionOn Sir Bani Yas Island the Google Car roof is visible almost everywhereDistinct scenery and the Google Car appears in nearly every panorama .
By Ismail.alghussein - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0, Wikimedia Commons

title: United States
content:
How to DistinguishBlue mailboxes sit on street corners.Speed-limit signs spell out SPEED LIMIT.If the inverted triangle sign says YIELD it is the United States; when the text is missing it is usually Canada.Double yellow centre lines are standard across both the United States and Canada.It is common to see rows of garbage bins set out in front of homes.Speed limits may be written in MPH.Signs you can findRepresentative Companies説明はページ下部。必ずしも道端で見つかるとは限りません。
Speed-limit signs show SPEED LIMIT . If the sign only reads SPEED you are probably in Oregon. Canada uses MAXIMUM.
In both the United States and Canada the centre of the road is often marked with double yellow lines .
YIELD and ONEWAY signs in the United States include the word itself . The left-hand examples are United States; the right-hand ones are Canada.
Look for the blue mailbox—Canada’s is red. When you find a post office, the state and city are often written somewhere on the building.
Many large companies are headquartered in the United States. Even utilities such as waste collection, electricity, and water authorities are listed companies, so you will see their logos everywhere. From left to right: Waste Management and Republic Services (waste haulers whose logos appear on bins), Chevron and Citgo (petrol stations), Target (retail) and more.
USA-themed GoodsNarrow down state/regionIslands linked to the United StatesThe United States governs or is otherwise involved with many islands far from the mainland.Hawaii & the Hawaiian IslandsAmerican SamoaU.S. Virgin IslandsMidway AtollNorthern Mariana IslandsPuerto Rico (an unincorporated territory with self-government)State-specific micro hintsRegion 1 (Northeast)Region 2 (Midwest)Region 3 (South)Region 4 (West)Clues for narrowing down the stateDecide first whether you are on an island, then use the licence plates, state-highway shields, the type of mountains and vegetation, how flat the terrain is, and the feel of the houses to narrow down the location. Some states have very distinctive clues.
① Whether cars have front plates and the plate colour can narrow down the state[Reference](by rumilb ). Start by memorising which states require a front plate.List of US licence plates (Wikipedia)States with “new” in the plate name often look yellowish(by niwaisound ).Characteristic colours by stateMostly black → DelawareMostly green → VermontDeep orange → New YorkAll yellow → AlaskaPale yellow overall or on both sides → MississippiWhite background with an orange panel on the right → UtahWhite background with an orange fruit in the centre → Florida / GeorgiaWhite background with a green tree in the centre → OregonWhite background with a red stripe along the top → OhioWhite background with a pale blue top band → Minnesota / Montana / IowaWhite background with blue at the top and yellow at the bottom → PennsylvaniaWhite background with reddish-brown at the top and blue at the bottom → IdahoWhite background with a faint red highlight near the top and a navy serial area → CaliforniaBright blue upper half and white lower half → Connecticut; Illinois can look similar.White top half with a light blue lower half → KentuckyFaint yellow on top and pale blue on the bottom → Washington?Front plate with a rainbow → Hawaii② Once you spot interstate or state-highway shields you can pinpoint your location.State-highway shields often give away the state. In some places the shield outline is the shape of the state itself.Interstate shields (blue signs with a red crown) use even numbers for east–west routes and odd numbers for north–south routes, with larger numbers in the northeast.Exits are numbered (“Exit 1”, etc.) and become visible when you zoom in.③ The Hawaiian Islands also have Street View, and the soil stands out as dark or reddishMemorise the states that do not require front plates (highlighted in yellow) before learning the plate colours. Lighting and angles can change the hue, so practice is essential. Vehicles move, so plates alone will not guarantee the state.
By Raccoonny - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Wikimedia Commons
They’re blurred but the colors might be helpful
by u/untoasted-bread69 in geoguessr The rules below are not perfectly reliable, but they help.United States numbered highwaysNorth–south routes have odd numbers that grow larger from east to west.East–west routes have even numbers that grow larger from north to south.Dwight D. Eisenhower National System of Interstate and Defense HighwaysNorth–south interstates use odd numbers that get smaller the farther west you go.East–west interstates use even numbers that get smaller the farther south you go.American Highways 101: Visual Guide to U.S. Road Sign Designs & Numbering Systems
Place names are distinctive, and the soil often looks dark or reddish.
Agricultural distributionIf you see corn, it is safer to look east of the Plains.Paddy fields with yellow warning stripes on utility poles point to northern California; without the stripes they are usually along the Mississippi River.Data source: U.S. Department of AgricultureCorn production is concentrated along the Corn Belt(参考文献 Corn: Production Acreage by County (USDA)). It is still grown nationwide, so corn alone does not guarantee the Midwest.
Soybeans share a footprint similar to corn(参考文献 Crop Production Maps (USDA)).
Rice fields cluster in specific parts of northern California and along the rivers and coast of Arkansas(参考文献 Crop Production Maps (USDA)).
Sugar cane grows in limited pockets around Florida; Louisiana has few roads through the cane fields(参考文献 Crop Production Maps (USDA)).
Wheat production does not overlap perfectly with corn or soybeans. The maps were adjusted so that the scales and colours match the other datasets.
Other tipsWhen you spawn inside a maze-like subdivision, avoid streets where you can see the back of a STOP sign—the larger road is usually the direction where the front of the sign faces(参考文献 First GeoGuessr Pinpoint Symposium – Dash techniques, memorising US state shields, CoopAOW 25k speedrun)ExampleYou can see the backside of STOP or “NO OUTLET” signs in all directions except southwest. Head southwest and you will soon see the front of a STOP sign to the east, guiding you towards a larger road.Petrol stations have different regional footprints(参考文献 10 Largest Gas Stations in the United States in 2023)Chevron → Gulf Coast or West Coast statesCitgo → Eastern statesPhillips 66 → Inland statesMurphy USA → Eastern statesTexaco → Gulf Coast or West Coast statesSome states can be identified from vehicle inspection stickers.A STOP sign is usually placed where a narrow street meets a wider one. If you can see the back of the sign you are heading towards a smaller road; seeing the front means you are approaching a larger road.
Avoid streets marked with NO OUTLET or DEAD END.
Hand-drawn map—treat as a rough guide only.
Some states can be recognised by the inspection sticker on the windscreen.
Representative companiesCompanyTickerOverviewIRDividend historyWaste ManagementWMMajor waste processor active in the United States, Canada, and Puerto Rico. Together with Republic Services it handles roughly half of America’s rubbish.NextEra EnergyNEELeading renewable-energy utility and the world’s largest generator of wind and solar power.Exxon MobilXOMOne of the supermajors among the international oil companies(参考文献 International petroleum majors).NVIDIANVDA-MicrosoftMSFT-S&P GlobalSPGIThe company behind the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average—benchmarks well known in Japan.FactSet Research SystemsFDSProvides financial-market data and related software. Publishes market-wide reports on FactSet Insight.

title: Uruguay
content:
How to DistinguishThe domain is .uyThe language is SpanishLicense plates are whiteSigns are mounted on white-painted wooden posts; bollards are usually white as wellThe entire country is pampa grassland used for cattle and sheep ranchingThe Google car looks blackUnlike Argentina, plates do not have a black centerWindbreaks or plantations with neatly aligned trees appear here and thereSome roads have double yellow center lines with white dashes spaced evenly in betweenMany signs use planks painted white with square holes . Bollards are also predominantly white; even yellow ones usually have white sides. License plates appear almost white , whereas Argentina uses plates with a dark center.
By Qqqqqq at English Wikipedia, CC BY-SA 3.0, Link
Center lines are distinctive . You will also see plantations where identical trees are planted in rows . Because Uruguay is flat with virtually no mountains, these rows likely serve as windbreaks or shelterbelts (参考文献 Looking back on life in Uruguay).
By CITY MVD - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link
The national oil company ANCAP operates most of the fuel network, so its stations are ubiquitous (参考文献 ANCAP - Uruguay). In Argentina, the comparable state company is YPF.
The Google car generally appears black. Within South America, a black car is virtually unique to Uruguay —but there are exceptions, so double-check other clues.
Simple fences and dirt tracks are also present but are less decisive—use them alongside the car color and signage.
Almost half of the country’s barley and soybean output comes from Soriano and Colonia in the southwest . Corn and rapeseed cluster in the west as well, so vast crop fields usually point in that direction (参考文献 Uruguay Production).
Narrow down city/townNear Cabo Polonio the black Google car is absent—do not place it outside Uruguay.Isla de Flores is covered on foot and features a prominent lighthouse(参考文献 Isla de Flores).Neither the settlement nor the access road uses the black Google car .
Street View here is a walking tour and includes a large lighthouse .
By Matero Uruguayo - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0, Link

title: Question 3
content:
ここはどこ？ JapanSouth KoreaMalaysiaHint: Malaysia drives on the left, while South Korea drives on the right.
Because the sign stands on the right side of the road, this is a right-driving country. Among these three choices, only South Korea drives on the right. Pay attention to which side the signs are on—they reveal the traffic side!

title: Region 3 (South)
content: Delaware, Maryland, the District of Columbia, West Virginia, Virginia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Georgia, Florida, Kentucky, Tennessee, Alabama, Mississippi, Arkansas, Oklahoma, Louisiana, and Texas.The Cherokee language is spoken in parts of North Carolina.Cherokee belongs to the Iroquoian language family and is used in parts of North Carolina and Oklahoma (参考文献 Cherokee language (ᏣᎳᎩ ᎦᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ)).

title: Question 4
content: ここはどこ？ NorwayUnited KingdomGermanyThe United Kingdom and regions influenced by it often drive on the left. By the way, this spot is Abbey Road, famous from the Beatles’ album cover.

title: Region 4 (West)
content:
Montana, Wyoming, Idaho, Colorado, Utah, Nevada, New Mexico, Arizona, Washington, Oregon, California, Alaska, and Hawaii.① Speed-limit signs that only show SPEED (without “limit”) usually mean Oregon.② Utility poles with three slender black wires point to California. In Hawaii, the pole typically carries a single thicker line.③ Snow poles on fire hydrants are common in cold states, especially Alaska.④ In Unalakleet, Alaska, the Google Car’s hat is visible.⑤ You can also spot the hat on the Google Car captured in Nome, Alaska.If the speed-limit sign only says SPEED, you are most likely in Oregon.
California poles often carry three slender black lines.
Hawaiian poles usually carry a single, thicker line.

title: Question 5
content: ここはどこ？ MacauHong KongTaiwanThe United Kingdom and its former territories often drive on the left and use yellow rear plates. Hong Kong, which was under British rule from 1841 to 1997, is no exception. For the same reason Sri Lanka and Kenya also use yellow rear license plates.

title: Question 6
content: ここはどこ？ United KingdomSouth AfricaNew ZealandThe URL on the truck ends with “.za,” which is the domain for South Africa. Spotting country-code domains is an easy way to lock in the correct answer. Every country has its own code, so learn them as you play!

title: Question 7
content: ここはどこ？ ABCPoint the compass north and look straight down to confirm whether the corner angles line up with the map. Unlike Japan, many intersections abroad have their own names. If the street or intersection names are unfamiliar, use those angles to verify your location!

title: Question 8
content: ここはどこ？ FranceItalySpainUnlike Japan, many countries lack guardrails along the roadside. Instead they install roadside posts called “bollards,” and each country has its own design. This thick white bollard is characteristic of France.

title: Question 9
content: ここはどこ？ ItalyTunisiaGreeceThe sign is written in Greek, so the answer is Greece. Mathematical symbols such as “Ελληνικά” originate from the Greek language. Text on signs can pinpoint a country or at least narrow it down to a language region such as the Spanish-speaking world.

title: Question 10
content: ここはどこ？ IsraelTunisiaJordanThe sign is in French, so the answer is Tunisia, a country with strong French influence. Tunisia and Senegal were once colonies of France. Former colonies and protectorates often retain the language and culture of their colonizer; besides France, many places still show traces of British rule.

title: Hokkaidō
content:
Toda la prefecturaLas carreteras muestran pivotes de guiado elevadosLas tiendas de conveniencia Seicomart son una seña de identidadEn invierno se instalan snow poles a lo largo de la calzadaLa arquitectura está adaptada al frío:Los tejados suelen ser planosEs frecuente ver garajes en forma de cascadaMuchas viviendas tienen un depósito de queroseno (home tank) en el exterior(参考文献 Sundia Home Tanks)Representative Companies説明はページ下部。必ずしも道端で見つかるとは限りません。
Las zonas con mucha nieve utilizan flechas suspendidas para señalar el centro de la vía. Existen en otras regiones frías , así que combínalas con otros indicios antes de afirmar que estás en Hokkaidō.
A la vera del camino crecen grandes macizos de fuki (Petasites japonicus). Aunque la planta existe en otras zonas, esa abundancia es típica de Hokkaidō.
Los postes muestran grandes números verticales en el lateral, lo que facilita distinguirlos .
La presencia de Seicomart confirma que estás en la red de la cadena.
En Hokkaidō abundan los depósitos de queroseno exteriores, los tejados totalmente planos (sistema snow-duct) y las cubiertas diseñadas para que la nieve caiga a zonas seguras. También es habitual encontrar escaleras para retirar la nieve del techo y garajes metálicos tipo cascade (参考文献 Características del garaje Cascade).

title: Getting Started with GeoGuessr
content:
This guide explains how to start playing GeoGuessr. You can play either in a desktop browser or via the smartphone app.
Visit the GeoGuessr website. Sign up with your Google account or another provider. Creating an account does not require entering credit-card or PayPal information, and you will not be charged. Set a password and choose a nickname. If you register with Gmail or a similar service, double-check that your nickname is not set to your real name. You can play for free 5 times every 15 minutes. However, if you join a lobby created by someone with a paid account, there is no time limit. Install GeoGuessr from Google Play or the Apple App Store. Complete the tutorial. It features landmarks like the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty. When you finish, confirm that your account name is not set to your Gmail address. Use the red buttons below to change the account name if needed.

title: Hiding Personal Information
content:
Because of how GeoGuessr is played, it is surprisingly easy to expose personal information by accident. These are the steps I currently take after a lot of trial and error.
When streaming I either use an incognito window or stay signed out of Google I force the current location to San Francisco from the developer tools Any text on screen that matches certain keywords (IP address, email address, etc.) is automatically replaced with placeholder characters Buttons I never want to press (for example Google’s “Update location” button) are hidden with CSS so they can’t be clicked Let me know if you have any other good ideas—I’d love to learn from them.
Changing the location Google Search usesIf you stay logged in to Google, the search results page can show very detailed addresses, so be careful. Even when signed out, Google can often guess the rough area from your IP address, so that deserves attention as well. Searching for transit lines, restaurants, or shops can also snap the map close to your IP-based location. When streaming I avoid searching for terms that point too close to home, such as “convenience store” or “supermarket.” You can change the detected location by using a VPN or by opening Chrome’s developer tools and overwriting the geolocation entirely.
Replacing any sensitive keywords in bulkWith some Chrome extensions (a simple self-made script works too) you can hide just the words you care about. If you configure it to rewrite email addresses, prefecture or city names, surnames, IP addresses, and even partial area codes, it becomes much harder to identify you even if something slips through. One approach is to use a plug-in that masks NG words so that place names are never rendered in the first place.
Extension ● TextForma
Hiding UI sections with CSSThis is mostly for peace of mind, but applying custom CSS to hide areas that might leak personal information—and the links that lead to them—can prevent mistakes because you simply cannot click them. The example below hides buttons that tend to show email addresses or area codes (working as of January 2023).
@-moz-document domain("google.co.jp") {div.qLP7kc {display: none;}div.qWuU9c {display: none;}div.scene-footer-container {bottom: 3em !important;right: 10em !important;margin-top: 0;transform: scale(1.5, 1.5);}div.sW9vGe {width: 12em;}img.gb_h {display: none;}div.gb_Ef {display: none;}div.hdeJwf{display: none;}div.etWJQ {display: none;}}@-moz-document url-prefix("https://www.google.com/maps/") {img.gb_h {display: none;}div.gb_Ef {display: none;}div.hdeJwf{display: none;}div.etWJQ {display: none;}}Before applying the CSSYou can read your email address and other account names from the top-right corner. Clicking “Send to your mobile device,” etc., also shows part of the area code.
After applying the CSSThe entire button area disappears so only the place description stays visible.
Some sites unexpectedly redirect to a Google login page, so you may want to blur that screen with CSS as well. Keep in mind that anything you blur can theoretically be restored, so add extra characters with :before or :after and change the string length if you need stronger masking.

title: Question 12
content: ここはどこ？ HungaryItalyPolandWarning signs edged with a thin red border are a hallmark of Poland. In GeoGuessr, signs that only exist in certain countries—road-number markers, arrows, or chevron-style boards—are powerful clues.

title: Question 13
content: ここはどこ？ PortugalRomaniaSpainPerforated concrete poles are typical in countries influenced by Portugal. For instance, Portuguese-speaking Brazil also uses poles with holes, and former Portuguese colonies such as São Tomé and Príncipe should show the same style. Utility poles are one of the best hints for identifying a country.

title: Question 14
content: ここはどこ？ NigeriaGhanaUgandaSometimes you can see the car that captured Google Street View. The equipment on that “Google Car” can reveal the country. A vehicle with a black tape wrap on the front-right corner, like this one, is characteristic of Ghana. Ghana, Guatemala, and Réunion have particularly recognizable cars. That said, any country might occasionally show a different car, so never assume a 100% identification from the vehicle alone.

title: Question 15
content: ここはどこ？ EstoniaUnited KingdomNetherlandsBrick houses are common in the Netherlands, Belgium, and northern Croatia. Among these options the Netherlands is the correct answer. Every country has its own traditional style, so architecture can help narrow down the region.

title: Question 16
content: ここはどこ？ NorwaySwedenFinlandShort white dashed shoulder lines are a characteristic of Sweden. The length, thickness, and color of road markings differ slightly by country, which helps you narrow down where you are.

title: Question 17
content: ここはどこ？ BotswanaEswatiniLesothoThis landscape, with very few trees and mostly grassland, is characteristic of Lesotho. Differences in elevation and climate create regions where trees do not grow well, areas full of conifers, or savannas, and those clues help you narrow down the country.

title: Question 18
content: ここはどこ？ Shetland Islands (United Kingdom)Canary Islands (Spain)Madagascar (Madagascar)The answer is the volcanic Canary Islands. Volcanic destinations such as Hawaii, the Pitcairn Islands, the Canaries, or Mount Etna in Sicily often have very dark soil. Coastal areas, by contrast, can look much lighter. Soil color is a useful clue for narrowing down the region.

title: Question 19
content: ここはどこ？ CzechiaSlovakiaBulgariaThe roadside bollards match those used in Czechia and Slovakia, but the answer here is Bulgaria. As this example shows, no single clue guarantees the right country 100% of the time in this game, so stay alert.

title: Oman
content:
How to DistinguishThe domain is .omTraffic keeps to the right-hand sideLicense plates are yellow or sometimes redSignposts are often painted with white-and-black stripesYellow plates dominate in urban areas . Taxis generally use red plates.
By Ji-Elle - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0, Link
Same source as above
Chevron signs usually have orange-and-black stripes . Directional signs and distance boards are blue. Poles with white-and-black striping are common in both Oman and the UAE .
In desert areas vegetation can be non-existent , or you may see sparse trees like these .
Date palms are concentrated along the north-eastern coast , and plantations are common . The Million Date Palm Plantation Project expanded cultivation nationwide (参考文献 Million Date Palm Plantation Project).
Pipeline signage at midstream sites often lists the destination .
There are several domestic energy companies. For example, Oman Oil Marketing (OOMCO) enjoys strong brand recognition .
Agricultural activity differs between regions. Coastal plains in the north grow vegetables , while fodder crops are cultivated inland .
The Musandam Peninsula—an exclave of Oman—also has Street View coverage . Some pockets have a Mediterranean climate with pomegranate orchards, but unlike Dhofar the mountains are rarely lush.
Southern Oman around Salalah receives monsoon rains. Hillsides can turn vivid green .
Other areas are dry. Unlike the north, there are fewer sheer cliffs and more scattered rocks. Livestock is common—expect to see cows , camels , goats, and pens used for herding (参考文献 Agriculture in Dhofar).
Large dune fields lie inland and toward the east (参考文献 Glennie, Kenneth W., et al. “Geological importance of luminescence dates in Oman and the Emirates: An overview.” Geochronometria 38 (2011): 259-271.). Selected imagery below was adapted from that reference.

Look for large rounded black rocks near the volcano —they are not numerous but stand out.

How to DistinguishThe country domain ends with .ye.Homes frequently use sun-dried bricks and stacked stone .
By Rod Waddington from Kergunyah, Australia - Taiz, Yemen, CC BY-SA 2.0, Link
Window frames and decorative elements tend to be ornate .
Coffee plantations are found here (参考文献 From Yemen, birthplace of mocha coffee).

title: Cyprus
content:
How to Distinguish Only southern Cyprus, where Greek Cypriots are the majority, is covered (as of Nov 2025) The domain is .cy Distinctive white bollards can be found Drive on the left Signs you can findYou can spot distinctive white bollards . Rare exceptions exist . Because traffic keeps left, signs are usually on the left side.
Signs are bilingual in English and Greek . Cyprus has remained in a state of conflict and has been divided since 1974 (参考文献 Republic of Cyprus), but Street View coverage exists only in the Greek-majority south.
Some vehicles have yellow rear plates . Signs and road markings sometimes follow the UK style.

Public Domain
Utility poles may have electric hazard plates or yellow plates .
Compared with Greece, chevrons are often red and white .

title: UK Sovereign Base Areas of Akrotiri and Dhekelia
content:
How to DistinguishThe domain is .cyDistinctive white bollards can be foundLeft-hand drivingSigns you can findDistinctive white bollards can be found . Since traffic is on the left, signs are placed on the left side.
Signage appears in English, Greek, and Turkish . Cyprus itself is in a state of conflict, having been divided into north and south since 1974 (参考文献 Republic of Cyprus).
Some vehicles have yellow rear license plates . Road signs and markings are sometimes those used in the United Kingdom.

title: Honduras
content:
How to DistinguishThe country domain is .hn.No official Google Street View coverage.Octagonal utility poles are commonly used.As in neighboring Mexico, octagonal utility poles are widespread .
License plates were green until around 2018 and have been blue since then.
CC0

title: Nicaragua
content: How to DistinguishThe country domain is .ni.There is no official Google Street View coverage.Black-and-white square bollards can be found .

title: French Guiana
content:
How to DistinguishNo official Google Street View coverage.You can spot bollards similar to those used in France.
Blue identification plates on utility poles are also present.

title: Guyana
content:
How to DistinguishNo official Google Street View coverage.Black license plates are common .
Rice is the principal crop; paddies stretch across the east of the country (参考文献 USDA - Rice Production). The photo below shows a Guyanese rice field.
A former British colony, so English is the official language.

title: Liberia
content: How to DistinguishNo official Street View.Official language: English.Founded by freed slaves returning from the United States; independent since 1847. The capital Monrovia is named after U.S. President Monroe(参考文献 MOFA (JP) Liberia).

title: Libya
content: How to DistinguishNo official Street View.A relatively high per‑capita GDP in the region (driven largely by oil), with comparatively modern urban infrastructure.Historical ties to Italy; sign styles often resemble Italian ones.

title: Mauritania
content:
How to DistinguishNo official Street View.Most of the country is desert; agriculture is largely limited to the Senegal River corridor.Less than about 0.5% of the land is arable; you are more likely to see pastoralism (e.g., camels) than farming (参考文献 AFP: Camel market).
Many houses are built from brick, rock, and straw .
Overall the terrain is flat, with exposed rocky arid plains .

title: Niger
content:
How to Distinguish No official Street View coverage As a former French colony, French utility poles and road signs can appear Almost the entire country is desert or steppe; trees are rarely seen except along the Niger River in the southeast The only river with year-round flow is the Niger River in the southwest, so areas with greenery like the photo are almost certainly along the river.
By Lars Rosendahl Appelquist - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

title: Suriname
content:
How to DistinguishNo official Google Street View coverage.The official language is Dutch.Many houses feature white-painted timber facades .
Formerly part of the Netherlands, so road signs use coloured borders just like Dutch signage . License plates are often yellow as well .

title: Togo
content: How to DistinguishNo official Street View.Official language: French.French‑style bollards and poles can be found.Hilly mountain roads appear in the interior.

title: Tristan da Cunha
content:
How to DistinguishNo official Google Street View coverage.The nearest inhabited island lies more than 2,000 km away(参考文献 Tristan da Cunha).A handful of sailings depart Cape Town for Tristan da Cunha each year (参考文献 Tristan da Cunha Website - Guide to Visits Section Pages).
CC0, link

title: Zambia
content:
How to DistinguishNo official Street View coverageAgriculture has expanded since the late 2000s and the food self-sufficiency rate exceeds 140%. Farms and center-pivot irrigation can be found (参考文献 Zambia agriculture overview (JP)).
Thatched-roof houses are common in rural areas.

title: Maldives
content: How to Distinguish No official Google Street View coverage. Street scenes from Dhidhdhoo (参考文献 Dhidhdhoo (Haa Alif Atoll)).

title: Algeria
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Country domain: .dzUtility poles similar to those in France (the former colonial ruler) can be found.
As in France, small yellow plates may show road numbers. Dashed lines can also appear on signs or along the road shoulder, which is similar to France.
The Atlas Mountains lie in the north and the Hoggar (Ahaggar) Mountains in the south, creating many rocky landscapes. The photo is likely the Hoggar range. The Mediterranean coast receives more rainfall and appears greener.

title: Benin
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Official language: French.Former colony of France; some poles and street-name signs resemble those in France.
Roughly 40% of the population is Christian, and around 17% practice Vodun (Voodoo). Sites like the Temple des Pythons (参考文献 Temple des Pythons) can be found.

title: Burkina Faso
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Country domain: .bfTraditional painted houses of the Gurunsi people (e.g., in Tiébélé) can sometimes be found .
As a former French colony, road signs often follow French formats.
By Ferdinand Reus – CC BY-SA 2.0

title: Cameroon
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Country domain: .cmOfficial languages: French and English.License plates are often orange (参考文献 Vehicle registration plates of Cameroon).
The northwest region was British Cameroon, and the southeast was French Cameroon. In the capital, French-style utility poles can be found.

title: Central African Republic
content: How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.The official languages are French and Sango.The CFA franc is the legal currency, and Bitcoin is also used.Formerly one of the territories of France as part of French Equatorial Africa (参考文献 French Equatorial Africa). You may spot French utility poles, but there are very few photos.

title: Chad
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023 there is no official coverage.French, Modern Standard Arabic, and several indigenous languages are in use.Formerly one of the countries that made up the France Community (参考文献 French Community). French remains widespread, and conversations in the cities are often held in French.
Public Domain, Link
Tuk-tuks are a common sight in urban areas.
By Abakar B - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link
A pipeline completed through Cameroon enables crude exports from the Doba oil field (参考文献 Chad–Cameroon Petroleum Development and Pipeline Project). Most of the country’s exports depend on this crude.
By Raminagrobis - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link
Narrow down state/regionThe farther north you go, the drier the desert climate becomes, while the south has a savanna climate with broad grasslands.

title: Côte d’Ivoire
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Independence from France in 1960; urban signage and poles often resemble France.
The country is the world’s top producer of cocoa (参考文献 article) (参考文献 article).

title: Ethiopia
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023 there is no official coverage.The country domain is .et.Amharic (አማርኛ), English, and five other official languages are used.Signs you can findRepresentative Companies説明はページ下部。必ずしも道端で見つかるとは限りません。
Amharic (አማርኛ) frequently appears on signage and storefronts.
Private taxis are typically painted blue and white (besides tuk-tuks there are standard cars), and license plates are generally red. Because Ethiopia levies high import duties to attract overseas businesses, it is considered difficult for individuals to buy vehicles manufactured abroad.
Four-wheeled vehicles are often Japanese brands, while many two-wheelers are made by Indian companies. The Isuzu vehicles shown in the photos are assembled locally in Ethiopia.
Traditional round houses can be spotted in rural areas (参考文献 Traditional Ethiopian houses and abandoned Japanese folk houses). Many photos show jar-like objects placed on the roofs, though their exact purpose is unclear. A variety of shapes exists beyond the ones shown here.
Overview of Representative CompaniesCompanyCodeDescriptionFinancialsDividend HistoryIsuzu Motors7202A major commercial vehicle manufacturer handling trucks and buses. In Japan it holds the top share in light, medium, and heavy truck sales, and worldwide it ranks second in the medium segment. The company also operates a plant in Ethiopia(参考文献 Japanese companies in Africa – AFRICA BUSINESS PARTNERS).

title: Gabon
content: How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Former French colony; French-style utility poles can be found.License plates are often black .

title: Guinea
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Private cars often use red plates, while taxis/commercial vehicles use black plates.CC0
CC0
One of Africa’s leading bauxite producers ; major mines are in the northwest and soils often look reddish. Export structure shown below (2019).

title: Guinea-Bissau
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Official language: Portuguese.Significant production of cashew nuts.Former Portuguese colony; Portuguese-speaking. Poles similar to those in Brazil and Sﾃ｣o Tomﾃｩ and Prﾃｭncipe can be found.
Many people work in cashew production; cashew trees and drying nuts can be seen, although Street View samples are limited.

title: Malawi
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.Country domain: .mwOfficial languages: Chichewa and English.Currency: Malawian kwacha (MWK).Independent from the United Kingdom in 1964. Some road signs resemble UK/Southern Africa styles.
Islands in Lake Malawi (e.g., Likoma) belong to Malawi (参考文献 Likoma District (JP)).

title: Moldova
content:
How to DistinguishNo official Street View coverage as of November 2023Country domain: .mdOfficial language: RomanianSigns you can findRomanian is widely used, just like in Romania.
The roadside bollards share the same style found in Romania.
House styles resemble northeastern Romania, with corrugated metal roofs in muted colours .
Yield signs are red and white but thinner than the Romanian design .

title: Morocco
content:
How to DistinguishAs of November 2023, there is no official coverage.The local “STOP” sign design is distinctive.
Utility poles similar to those in France can be found . Much of Morocco was a French protectorate until 1956.
Road signs often include Arabic and French; Berber (Amazigh) script is also seen (参考文献 Berber languages).

title: Mozambique
content: How to DistinguishNo official Google Street View coverage as of November 2023.Japan imports coal and liquefied natural gas from Mozambique, resulting in a trade deficit with the country (参考文献 Mozambique trade and investment information (JETRO)). Companies such as Mozal, which receive investment from Japanese firms, have grown into major domestic corporations, highlighting the depth of Japan–Mozambique ties.

content: How to DistinguishThe domain is .aqThe scenery is distinctive

title: Azerbaijan
content:
How to DistinguishThere is currently no official Street View coverage, so it does not appear in GeoGuessr.Azerbaijani—related to Turkish—is the official language.Signs you can findAzerbaijani is the primary language used.
Known for the Baku oil fields, the country is lined with gas and crude pipelines. As of 2019, more than 90% of exports were resource related (see chart below).
License plates often feature a small national flag in the upper-left corner.
CC0
Road surfaces are relatively flat. The barriers resemble those in Kazakhstan, Turkey, or Germany, and it’s unclear which style is most common .

How to DistinguishNo Google Street View—official or otherwise—exists for these territories.Map of the French Southern Territories and Antarctic Lands, administered by France. The domain covers numerous uninhabited islands from the seas around Madagascar down towards Antarctica, though not every claim is widely recognised (参考文献 Explore All Countries – French Southern and Antarctic Lands).

title: San Andrés, Providencia and Santa Catalina
content:
How to Distinguish The domain is .co. Sign backs carry the same cross braces used on mainland Colombian signs. As Colombian territory, license plates are yellow. A black Google car indicates Providencia. A white Google car indicates San Andrés. Vehicles are yellow-plated with a black Google car.
Look for the white Google car with yellow Colombian plates.

title: Sierra Leone
content:
How to DistinguishCountry domain: .slOfficial language: EnglishFreetown lies on a peninsula with the Sierra Leone Mountains; houses on slopes are visible even from the city center . Outside the capital, mangrove areas are widespread.
Rice is the staple food, but production is insufficient and imports are needed (参考文献 WFP (JP)).

title: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
content:
How to DistinguishThe territory domain is .gs.In Grytviken you can see rusted, abandoned buildings.Seals and penguins are commonly present.At Hercules Bay you travel by boat.Grytviken once hosted whaling facilities (参考文献 Grytviken), and you can still find rusted buildings left behind .
You may encounter seals or penguins .

title: Turkmenistan
content:
How to DistinguishThere is currently no official Street View, so it does not appear in GeoGuessr.The country domain is .tm.The United Nations General Assembly has recognized Turkmenistan as a permanently neutral state.Signs you can findRoughly 70% of the territory is covered by the Karakum Desert (参考文献 Karakum Desert).
The Darvaza gas crater is located here .

title: Uzbekistan
content:
How to DistinguishNo official Street View, so it is not currently in GeoGuessr.The domain is .uz.Samarkand Blue tiles—brilliant turquoise mosaics—decorate mosques and madrasas throughout the country (参考文献 Samarkand – Crossroads of Cultures).
Windows, arches, and doors often feature pale blue trim . Traditional homes hide their courtyards behind high walls, making them invisible from the street (参考文献 Stories from Uzbek Courtyards).

title: About This Site
content:
Contact nanjakorewa0***gmail.com (please replace *** with @) · DM on X · GitHub issues.
Feel free to reference or mention the contents of this site during streams or other activities—I genuinely appreciate the shout-out.
Frequently asked questions Q. May I use the content of this website for education? A. Of course. Q. Some pages do not reflect information added after 2024. A. Most of the pages were written in 2023 while I was streaming. At that time even resources such as plonkit had not gathered tips for every country, and similar references in Japanese simply did not exist. I built this site to organise my own notes, so there are still places where information—such as 2025 Google Car updates—has not been refreshed yet. Q. Could you add Street View screenshots? A. Google’s terms forbid extracting Street View imagery, so I only use public-domain assets and similar resources. Overview I started playing GeoGuessr around February 2023.
This page works as a notebook of interesting spots that caught my eye during games—almost like saving screenshots. If you notice mistakes or have suggestions, please let me know via the comment section or GitHub issues. Including the original source in your message helps a lot, and I will credit it in the following format.
Example) こんにちは(by nanjakorewa )こんばんは(by Geotips )さようなら(参考文献 『左様なら』)
Everything about countries, regions, cities, and history is written purely for enjoying GeoGuessr, and I aim to stay politically neutral. The maps shown here use the in-game depiction of borders for convenience and may differ from real-world administrative or political boundaries.
For images labelled CC-BY-, please see Creative Commons licences. GFDL indicates the GNU Free Documentation License. If you find any image (other than public-domain material) without proper attribution, please let me know here.
Images sourced from USDA.gov are linked according to their Policies and Links. Most road signs on this site are public-domain illustrations. A handful of pages—such as Latvia—include credited images. Corporate products and logos appear strictly for reference under fair-use considerations and do not imply sponsorship. If you spot any problematic usage, please reach out via the comment field or contact options above and I’ll address it promptly. Other quotations follow Article 32, paragraph 1 of Japan’s Copyright Act.
Displayed maps may omit remote islands or tiny regions for layout reasons, and Street View locations may differ from the shapes shown on the map. Please note that this is a personal memo, so I may occasionally jot down topics that are not strictly GeoGuessr-related.
